evals.name: react-basic-setup-with-mutable-data
evals.prompt: I'm building an admin dashboard in a fresh Vite + React 18 + TypeScript project. I need to show a list of users in a table — columns: id (number), email (text), role (text, one of 'admin' | 'editor' | 'viewer'), and status (text, 'active' or 'inactive'). The data is around 5,000 users that I get back from `/api/users` (already loaded into a `users: User[]` state in my parent component). Users get added or removed when I submit other forms. I want to integrate gp-grid for this. Walk me through what to install, what CSS to import, and the actual component code — make sure data updates from form submissions are efficient (no full rebuilds). Don't worry about styling beyond defaults.
evals.expected_output: A correct React integration that: (1) installs @gp-grid/react, (2) imports the CSS once at app entry, (3) wraps the grid in a sized container, (4) uses useGridData with getRowId for mutation efficiency (NOT just rowData=users), (5) sets cellDataType correctly per column, (6) hooks up addRows/removeRows/updateRow on form callbacks. Should not use raw rowData prop for mutations on 5000 rows.
assertions: Recommends installing @gp-grid/react (the React wrapper, not @gp-grid/core alone)
Tells the user to import the stylesheet from @gp-grid/react/dist/styles.css exactly once at app entry
Wraps the grid in a parent element with explicit width AND height (not relying on auto sizing)
Uses the useGridData hook from @gp-grid/react for mutable data — does NOT recommend rebuilding the rowData prop on every state change
Provides getRowId callback to useGridData so transactions can identify rows
Demonstrates calling addRows / removeRows / updateRow on form submission events instead of replacing the full array
Sets cellDataType='number' for the id column and cellDataType='text' for email/role/status
Imports the component as Grid (or GpGrid) from @gp-grid/react
Solution code would compile against the real @gp-grid/react public API (no invented props/types/exports)

evals.name: vue-custom-cell-renderer
evals.prompt: I have a Vue 3 + Vite + TypeScript app with gp-grid already wired up showing orders. Order shape: `{ id: number; customer: string; total: number; status: 'pending' | 'shipped' | 'delivered' | 'cancelled' }`. Right now the 'status' column shows raw text. I want to render it as a colored pill: pending=orange, shipped=blue, delivered=green, cancelled=red. Show me how to add a custom cell renderer for just the status column. I'd prefer the renderer as its own .vue SFC if that's idiomatic.
evals.expected_output: A Vue custom renderer using a Vue SFC component (.vue file) passed directly as the `cellRenderer` field on the column (since @gp-grid/vue's ColumnDefinition widens the renderer to accept Components). The SFC should accept CellRendererParams as props. Should NOT recommend the registry-by-string pattern as the only option without mentioning the SFC option exists. Should mention container sizing, CSS import only if relevant.
assertions: Defines the renderer as a separate .vue Single-File Component (per the user's preference)
Passes the imported SFC directly via cellRenderer: SfcComponent on the status column (no registry-by-string forced when SFC is requested)
Imports ColumnDefinition from @gp-grid/vue (NOT from @gp-grid/core), so the renderer field accepts a Vue Component
The SFC uses defineProps to accept the CellRendererParams shape (value, rowData, column, etc.)
Maps each status value to the requested color: pending→orange, shipped→blue, delivered→green, cancelled→red
Pill styling is reasonable (padding, rounded corners, contrasting text color)
Solution code would compile against the real @gp-grid/vue public API (no invented props/types/exports)

evals.name: angular-server-side-data-source
evals.prompt: Angular 19 standalone-component app. I need to show a 2M-row dataset of trades from a backend. The backend exposes GET /api/trades with query params: ?page=N&pageSize=N&sortBy=field:dir,field2:dir&filter_<field>=<value>. Returns `{ items: Trade[]; total: number }`. Trade shape: `{ id: number; symbol: string; side: 'buy' | 'sell'; quantity: number; price: number; tradedAt: string }`. Show me how to wire up gp-grid with a server-side data source so the backend handles paging, sorting, and filtering. Use Angular's HttpClient and signals where natural. The grid should fill the full page below a 56px-tall toolbar.
evals.expected_output: Angular integration using @gp-grid/angular's GpGridComponent, createServerDataSource with a fetch fn that builds URLSearchParams matching the backend's contract, returns { rows, totalRows }. Uses HttpClient with firstValueFrom or similar to make it async. Shows correct AngularColumnDefinition[] with appropriate cellDataType per field. Container has explicit height (e.g. calc(100vh - 56px)). Standalone imports include GpGridComponent. CSS import noted. Data source created once (not per render).
assertions: Imports GpGridComponent from @gp-grid/angular and lists it in the component's standalone imports array
Uses createServerDataSource from @gp-grid/angular (NOT createClientDataSource or rowData)
The fetch function builds query params matching the backend contract: page, pageSize, sortBy=col:dir,col:dir, filter_<field>=<value>
Fetch returns the correct shape { rows, totalRows } from the response { items, total }
Uses Angular HttpClient (with firstValueFrom or async/await pattern)
Container has explicit height that fills the page below the 56px toolbar (e.g. calc(100vh - 56px) or flex-1 inside a sized parent)
Imports the @gp-grid/angular CSS once (in styles.css or angular.json)
Uses AngularColumnDefinition[] for the columns array and sets correct cellDataType per Trade field (number for id/quantity/price, text for symbol/side, text or dateString for tradedAt)
The data source is created once (class field or ngOnInit), not recreated on every change detection
Solution code would compile against the real @gp-grid/angular public API (no invented inputs/types/exports)

evals.name: react-multi-select-edit-renderer
evals.prompt: Working in a React 18 + Vite + TypeScript app. I have a gp-grid showing tasks. Each task has a `tags: string[]` field — possible values are 'urgent', 'important', 'archived', 'review'. I want users to double-click the tags cell and get a multi-select dropdown to pick 0..N tags. On commit, I need to PATCH /api/tasks/:id with the new tags. Build me the custom edit renderer (you can use react-select or just plain checkboxes if simpler — your call). Make sure the cell shows the tags nicely when not editing too.
evals.expected_output: A complete React solution that: (1) defines a cell renderer for the tags column showing tags as visible chips/badges (handles empty array), (2) defines an edit renderer that opens a multi-select on edit (react-select with menuPortalTarget=document.body, OR plain checkboxes — both acceptable), (3) wires onValueChange/onCommit/onCancel correctly, (4) sets editable: true on the column, (5) provides getRowId, (6) wires onCellValueChanged to PATCH the backend with the new tags. Edit renderer must use defineProps/state correctly and handle Escape→onCancel, Enter→onCommit. Does NOT mutate state from outside the renderer hooks rules.
assertions: Defines a cell renderer for the tags column that displays the array as visible chips/badges (and handles the empty array case)
Defines an edit renderer that opens a multi-select UI on edit (react-select OR plain checkboxes are both acceptable)
The edit renderer calls params.onValueChange(newValue) when the user changes the selection
The edit renderer handles Escape key by calling params.onCancel()
The edit renderer commits via params.onCommit() (on Enter, blur, or done button)
Sets editable: true on the tags column
Provides getRowId so onCellValueChanged can identify the row
Wires onCellValueChanged to PATCH /api/tasks/:id with the new tags array
If react-select is used: passes menuPortalTarget={document.body} (or equivalent) to escape the cell's overflow:hidden clipping
Renderers wired via the cellRenderers / editRenderers registry props (or inline column functions); column references the renderer by string key when registry is used
Solution code would compile against the real @gp-grid/react public API (no invented props/types/exports)
